title: Tunnel Bell
desc: A tunnel-mouth warning bell needs tending before the lake's peaceful silence turns deceptive.

steps.text:
One of the watched tunnel mouths lies in a cove so calm it looks harmless on purpose. Reeds whisper at the bank. The lower stones stay cold even in daylight. Above them, Najarin's castle keeps its lantern glow over the lake, and below them the water darkens into a green that never quite becomes transparent. The warning bell here is simple: a bronze float rig tied to the submerged guide line. If the line moves, the bell speaks. If weed and silt choke it, the lake keeps the sound to itself.

The post is empty for the moment except for a maintenance note tucked under the bell housing. Clear the bell line and float rig, or reset the chain markers on the stair that descends toward the watched water.

options.label: Clear the warning bell line and float rig
Reset the chain markers on the tunnel stair

steps.text:
The bell line comes up cold, roped with weed and black silt from the shallows. You strip it clean section by section, free the bell tongue, and rinse the bronze until it can answer quickly again. When you test the line, the note that rolls out is clear and narrow, the kind of sound that travels a long way over still water. It carries across the cove, brushes the castle stone, and returns as a softened echo.

For a moment everything holds still around it: reeds, ripples, the dim lights above the shore. Then the lake goes quiet again, but it is the right kind of quiet now, the sort that has chosen watchfulness instead of neglect.

options.label: Set the float back among the reeds and leave the bell live

steps.text:
The stair to the tunnel mouth drops in wet stone steps toward a green-black opening below the surface. The chain markers along the rail are there to keep the descent honest when mist, darkness, or nerves start changing the shape of distance. You reseat one leaning post, draw the chain taut again, and scrape the moss from the step edges until the line down reads clearly from the bank.

When you stand at the top afterward, the whole descent has a severe kind of grace to it. The lake reflects a strip of pale sky and the first lanterns from Najarin's castle, while the tunnel below keeps its own counsel. The path between them is no longer careless. It is prepared.

options.label: Brush the last moss aside and reopen the stair
